title: Viola cheeseana
intro: Viola cheesemanii, commonly known as the Kermadec pansy, is a species of flowering plant in the violet family (Violaceae) native to New Zealand. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Viola cheesemanii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Viola cheesemanii is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to alleviate colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Viola cheeseana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Violaceae, genus Viola. It is native to China and is also known as the Yunnan violet.
